content.text: Answer FOUR questions in detail. State carefully any results used without proof.

problem.text:
Let \((s_n:n>0)\) be a bounded real sequence and write \(\sigma_n=(s_1+\cdots+s_n)/n\) for each \(n>0\). Prove that
\[
\limsup_n \sigma_n \leq \limsup_n s_n.
\]
Hence, or otherwise, show that if \(s_n\to s\), then \(\sigma_n\to s\) also.
Let \(X\) be a metric space; let \(A\subseteq X\) be dense and let \(U\subseteq X\) be open. Prove that \(U\subseteq\overline{A\cap U}\), where the overline denotes closure.
Let \(A\) be a subset of the metric space \(X\) and let \(f:X\to\mathbb{R}\) be continuous.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(A\) is compact, then \(f\) is bounded on \(A\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show by example that if instead \(A\) is closed and bounded, then \(f\) can fail to be bounded on \(A\).

problem.text: Let \(f:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) be continuous; assume that \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}f(n)=-1\) and that \(\limsup_{n\to\infty}f(n)=+1\). Prove that \(f\) has infinitely many zeros.

Let the function \(f:(0,1)\to\mathbb{R}\) be differentiable. Prove that if its derivative \(f':(0,1)\to\mathbb{R}\) is bounded, then \(f\) is the restriction to \((0,1)\) of a continuous function \(F:[0,1]\to\mathbb{R}\).
